title: Hemiphlebium apodum
taxonomy: Hemiphlebium apodum is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It is native to Asia and has been found in countries such as China, India, and Sri Lanka. This species of fern belongs to the genus Hemiphlebium, which is a part of the family Thelypteridaceae. The classification of Hemiphlebium apodum is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, and order Polypodiales. Hemiphlebium apodum is also known by its synonyms, including Hemiphlebium pectinatum and Hemiphlebium pectinatum var. apodum.

intro: Hemiphlebium apodum, also known as Hemiphlebium, is a species of fern in the family Hemiphlebiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. Bioactive constituents of Hemiphlebium include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Hemiphlebium is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of extracts, tablets, and capsules to treat various health conditions.
